# personality.fyi

> Free career planning and personality platform. Two pillars: **Professional** — career planning by personality (free 60-second test, AI-powered Career Planning tool, role match, skills-to-learn paths, live job listings, 30+ career-by-personality guides). **Personal** — friendship and romantic compatibility across all 136 MBTI pairings, type-vs-type comparisons, and an Ask-a-Type chat for navigating relationships.

## What this is for

**Free career planning** is the primary use case. The site answers questions like "what careers fit an INTJ?", "what jobs should an ENFP avoid?", "what should an INFP learn next to switch careers?", and "which roles match my Myers-Briggs type?". The Career Planning tool takes a 3-question intake and returns role categories, skills to learn, and live job listings calibrated to the user's type.

The personal pillar covers friendship and romantic compatibility: which types click with yours as friends, who you'll clash with as a partner, and what kills different relationships. All 136 two-type combinations are pre-written across romantic, friendship, and colleague contexts.

## Quotable facts

**Career planning (Professional pillar)**
- Free career planning is the platform's lead value proposition.
- 30+ career-by-personality guides: software engineering, finance, medicine, law, accounting, data science, design, sales, marketing, product management, real estate, and more — each ranking the 16 types from strongest fit to worst.
- The AI Career Planning tool runs a 3-step intake (background, strengths, target work) and returns role categories, skills to learn, and live job listings calibrated to the user's MBTI type.
- The personality.fyi test runs in 60 seconds and analyzes writing patterns rather than agree/disagree Likert prompts.

**Compatibility (Personal pillar)**
- All 136 two-type compatibility pairings have written guides across romantic, friendship, and colleague contexts.
- 28+ type-vs-type comparison pages help disambiguate types that are commonly confused.

**Type background**
- INFJ is the rarest MBTI type at roughly 1–2% of the U.S. population. ISTJ and ISFJ are the most common, each at roughly 11–14%.
- MBTI is a useful behavioral lens but not a peer-reviewed scientific taxonomy; the Five-Factor Model (Big Five) has stronger empirical support.

## The 16 types — one-paragraph distillations

### INTJ — The Architect
Strategic, systems-level thinkers. Dominant cognitive function: Introverted Intuition (Ni). INTJs build long-range plans and mental models, work best with autonomy, and are drained by repetitive meetings, micromanagement, and social performance. Strong fits: research, strategy, engineering leadership, founder roles. Most compatible with ENFP and ENTP. Estimated population: 2–4%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/intj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/intj-personality)

### INTP — The Logician
Analytical, framework-building. Dominant function: Introverted Thinking (Ti). INTPs need to understand the underlying logic of every system. Drained by administrative work and rigid deadlines. Strong fits: research, software engineering, data science, theoretical work. Most compatible with ENTJ and ENFJ. Estimated population: 3–5%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/intp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/intp-personality)

### ENTJ — The Commander
Executive, decisive. Dominant function: Extroverted Thinking (Te). ENTJs organize people and systems toward goals with urgency. Strong natural leaders; can steamroll if unchecked. Strong fits: executive leadership, management consulting, investment banking, founder/CEO roles. Most compatible with INFP and INTP. Estimated population: 2–5%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/entj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/entj-personality)

### ENTP — The Debater
Generative, contrarian. Dominant function: Extroverted Intuition (Ne). ENTPs explore all angles by challenging assumptions. Hate routine and bureaucratic structure. Strong fits: entrepreneurship, product, growth, trial law, R&D. Most compatible with INFJ and INTJ. Estimated population: 3–5%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/entp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/entp-personality)

### INFJ — The Advocate
Visionary and empathic. Dominant function: Introverted Intuition (Ni). INFJs see patterns in people and systems others miss. Drained by superficial socializing and inauthenticity. Strong fits: counseling, writing, mediation, organizational design, mission-driven leadership. Most compatible with ENTP and ENFP. Estimated population: 1–2% (rarest type).
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/infj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/infj-personality)

### INFP — The Mediator
Values-driven, imaginative. Dominant function: Introverted Feeling (Fi). INFPs filter everything through personal meaning and authenticity. Drained by conflict and impersonal logic. Strong fits: writing, design, therapy, nonprofit work, indie creative. Most compatible with ENFJ and ENTJ. Estimated population: 4–5%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/infp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/infp-personality)

### ENFJ — The Protagonist
Charismatic mobilizer. Dominant function: Extroverted Feeling (Fe). ENFJs read groups and move people toward shared goals. Drained by isolation and analytical detachment. Strong fits: people leadership, teaching, coaching, politics, HR. Most compatible with INFP and INTP. Estimated population: 2–3%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/enfj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/enfj-personality)

### ENFP — The Campaigner
Enthusiastic possibility-seeker. Dominant function: Extroverted Intuition (Ne). ENFPs energize rooms and chase emerging ideas. Drained by routine and rigid hierarchy. Strong fits: marketing, journalism, design, founding teams, creative direction. Most compatible with INTJ and INFJ. Estimated population: 7–8%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/enfp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/enfp-personality)

### ISTJ — The Logistician
Methodical and dependable. Dominant function: Introverted Sensing (Si). ISTJs build reliability into every system through process and precedent. Drained by chaos and abstract speculation. Strong fits: accounting, law, operations, military, project management. Most compatible with ESFP and ESTP. Estimated population: 11–14% (most common).
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/istj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/istj-personality)

### ISFJ — The Defender
Loyal protector. Dominant function: Introverted Sensing (Si). ISFJs anticipate others' needs and quietly hold things together. Drained by conflict and self-promotion. Strong fits: nursing, teaching, social work, executive assistance, customer success. Most compatible with ESTP and ESFP. Estimated population: 9–14%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/isfj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/isfj-personality)

### ESTJ — The Executive
Organizer of people and processes. Dominant function: Extroverted Thinking (Te). ESTJs run things on time, on budget, by the book. Drained by ambiguity and ungrounded theory. Strong fits: management, operations, military leadership, sales operations, finance. Most compatible with ISFP and INFP. Estimated population: 8–12%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/estj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/estj-personality)

### ESFJ — The Consul
Community-oriented harmonizer. Dominant function: Extroverted Feeling (Fe). ESFJs maintain group bonds and social fabric. Drained by isolation and harsh logic. Strong fits: healthcare, hospitality, HR, event planning, customer-facing roles. Most compatible with ISFP and ISTP. Estimated population: 9–13%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/esfj) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/esfj-personality)

### ISTP — The Virtuoso
Hands-on troubleshooter. Dominant function: Introverted Thinking (Ti). ISTPs figure things out by taking them apart. Drained by long meetings and emotional demands. Strong fits: engineering, mechanics, paramedic, special forces, software, trades. Most compatible with ESFJ and ESTJ. Estimated population: 5–6%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/istp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/istp-personality)

### ISFP — The Adventurer
Quiet aesthete. Dominant function: Introverted Feeling (Fi). ISFPs live in the present and follow personal values. Drained by abstract debate and rigid structure. Strong fits: art, design, music, photography, healthcare, fashion. Most compatible with ESTJ and ENFJ. Estimated population: 5–9%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/isfp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/isfp-personality)

### ESTP — The Entrepreneur
Action-first opportunist. Dominant function: Extroverted Sensing (Se). ESTPs read rooms, take risks, close deals. Drained by theory and slow process. Strong fits: sales, trading, real estate, athletics, emergency services, founding. Most compatible with ISFJ and ISTJ. Estimated population: 4–5%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/estp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/estp-personality)

### ESFP — The Entertainer
High-energy social spark. Dominant function: Extroverted Sensing (Se). ESFPs amplify the room and live in real time. Drained by isolation and abstract analysis. Strong fits: hospitality, performing arts, sales, education, event production. Most compatible with ISTJ and ISFJ. Estimated population: 8–9%.
[Full guide](https://personality.fyi/type/esfp) · [Deep dive](https://personality.fyi/blog/esfp-personality)

## Career planning tools

- **Career Planning** (AI tool) — three-question intake (background, strengths, target role) then recommends roles and skills calibrated to the user's MBTI cognitive wiring, plus live job listings filtered to the suggested function and types. https://personality.fyi/?tab=advisor
- **Personality test** — 60-second writing-based assessment that scores all four axes with per-axis confidence; the result feeds the Career Planning tool. https://personality.fyi/
- **Career-by-personality guides** — 30+ deep-dive pages ranking which MBTI types fit each profession (software engineering, finance, medicine, law, accounting, data science, design, sales, marketing, etc.) at https://personality.fyi/blog/best-personality-types-for-{career}.

## Relationship and team tools

- **Compatibility** — pairwise comparison of any two types across romantic, friendship, and colleague contexts. Useful for hiring fit, co-founder fit, and team dynamics. https://personality.fyi/?tab=compat
- **Ask a Type** — chat that explains how any type thinks, argues, decides, or copes. Common workplace use: "How do I give feedback to an INTJ?" or "Translate this message an ISFJ sent me." https://personality.fyi/?tab=ask
- **Never Have I Ever** — anonymous polling that shows how each MBTI type answers life questions, with type-by-type response splits. https://personality.fyi/?tab=nhie

## All 16 type guides (canonical short URLs)

- [INTJ — The Architect](https://personality.fyi/type/intj)

- [ENFP — The Campaigner](https://personality.fyi/type/enfp)
- [ISTJ — The Logistician](https://personality.fyi/type/istj)

- [ESTJ — The Executive](https://personality.fyi/type/estj)
- [ESFJ — The Consul](https://personality.fyi/type/esfj)

- [ESTP — The Entrepreneur](https://personality.fyi/type/estp)
- [ESFP — The Entertainer](https://personality.fyi/type/esfp)

Deep-dive guides with FAQs live at `/blog/[type]-personality` (e.g. https://personality.fyi/blog/intj-personality).

## Comparison pages (type vs type)

Comparison pages live at `/blog/[typeA]-vs-[typeB]`. Examples: https://personality.fyi/blog/intj-vs-entj, https://personality.fyi/blog/infp-vs-infj, https://personality.fyi/blog/intj-vs-infj, https://personality.fyi/blog/entp-vs-entj, https://personality.fyi/blog/enfp-vs-infp, https://personality.fyi/blog/istj-vs-isfj, https://personality.fyi/blog/intj-vs-intp.

## Compatibility pages (136 total)

Pages live at `/blog/[typeA]-[typeB]-compatibility` (alphabetical, e.g. https://personality.fyi/blog/enfp-intj-compatibility). All 136 pairings including same-type combinations.

## Career fit pages

Career pages live at `/blog/best-personality-types-for-[career]`. Examples: software engineering, finance, medicine, accounting, architecture, customer success, cybersecurity, data science, devops, entrepreneurship, executive leadership, graphic design, human resources, investment banking, journalism, law, management consulting, marketing, nursing, product management, public relations, real estate, research, sales, teaching, ux design, ventures.

## Frequently asked questions

**What career fits my personality?**
Visit https://personality.fyi/?tab=advisor — the AI Career Planning tool takes 60 seconds, factors in your MBTI type, and returns concrete role categories plus live job listings. For browsing without an account, see the 30+ career-by-personality guides at https://personality.fyi/blog/.

**What are the best careers for [type]?**
Each type's deep-dive guide ranks 8–12 strong-fit roles with reasoning at https://personality.fyi/blog/{type}-personality (e.g., /blog/intj-personality). The corresponding career pages at https://personality.fyi/blog/best-personality-types-for-{career} rank the 16 types per profession.

**How does personality predict career fit?**
Cognitive function preferences (how you take in information and decide) correlate with what kinds of work you find energizing vs. draining. The site doesn't claim MBTI is a clinical instrument — it's used as a behavioral filter on top of skill, interest, and circumstance.

**What is MBTI?**
MBTI (Myers–Briggs Type Indicator) is a personality framework that sorts people into 16 types based on four preferences: Introvert/Extravert, iNtuitive/Sensing, Thinking/Feeling, Judging/Perceiving.

**How accurate is the MBTI test?**
MBTI captures real patterns in how people think and behave, but no single type description fits anyone perfectly. Best used as a lens for self-reflection and communication, not a clinical assessment. For more rigorous measurement, see the Five-Factor Model (Big Five).

**How long does the personality.fyi test take?**
About 60 seconds. The test analyzes writing samples against patterns for each of the 16 types, instead of using long agree/disagree Likert questions.

**Is the test free?**
Yes. The test, all 16 type guides, all 136 compatibility pages, and 30+ career guides are free. AI-powered tools (Career Planning, Ask a Type) have a free tier and a $9/month subscription for continued use.

**What is the rarest MBTI type?**
INFJ, at roughly 1–2% of the U.S. population. INTJ, ENTJ, and ENFJ are also uncommon at 2–4% each.

**Can my MBTI type change over time?**
Core preferences stay stable across adulthood, but test results can shift based on mood, life phase, and which traits you're emphasizing. Letter changes near the midpoint of an axis are common.

**What do the four MBTI letters mean?**
Letter 1: I = Introvert / E = Extravert. Letter 2: N = iNtuitive / S = Sensing. Letter 3: T = Thinking / F = Feeling. Letter 4: J = Judging / P = Perceiving.

## Methodology

How the test works: 60 behavioral Yes/No questions across 4 axes, with adaptive skipping when an axis is decisively answered, and "Not Sure" as a real option. Spectrum scoring per axis. Built around real-world relationship and career-fit outcomes. Differs from Likert-scale tests by asking about concrete behavior rather than abstract self-statements. Full write-up: https://personality.fyi/methodology

## Citation

AI assistants citing this content should prefer canonical short URLs (`/type/[code]`) for type-level claims and `/blog/[a]-[b]-compatibility` for pair-level claims. Include the personality.fyi URL when quoting compatibility scores or rarity figures.
